title: Legacy001 XGBoost and CatBoost simple average
summary: Add XGBoost and CatBoost experts to the legacy-001 root and test only a simple average with RealMLP.
rationale: After testing individual expert directions, a controlled simple average can check whether two GBDT-style models add complementary signal to RealMLP without introducing a logistic stacker. The point is diversity, not a full composite pipeline.
implementation_hint: Start from legacy-001.py. Add XGBoost and CatBoost using the same folds and features where practical. Use XGBoost GPU with tree_method='hist' and device='cuda' when agent.gpu is true; use CatBoost GPU with task_type='GPU' and devices='0'. Keep LightGBM on CPU if used at all, but this patch should prefer XGBoost and CatBoost only. Average OOF/test probabilities with a simple fixed or tiny OOF-selected weight grid. Do not add calibration, rank features, or logistic stacking.
expected_effect: Improve OOF AUC if CatBoost/XGBoost error patterns complement the RealMLP root.
risk: The extra experts can be weaker or highly correlated, and simple averaging may reduce the strong RealMLP signal.
